The key idea of the VMD-Net network design is to leverage intra-frame and inter-frame correspondences for video mirror detection.

VMD-Net utilizes low-level features at the 2nd scale and high-level features at the 5th scale, similar to the design of DeepLabV3 which feeds high-level features to an atrous spatial pyramid pooling module.

VMD-Net assigns a dual correspondence module to both low-level and high-level features to produce intermediate and final outputs, while the DC Module has two stages aiming to learn intra-frame and short-term inter-frame correspondences in input images.
The DC Module focuses on extracting long-term inter-frame correspondences across all input images to enhance the learning of temporal correspondence, enabling VMD-Net to exploit correspondences.
